name: Japan Open
stakes: This week's Japan Open triumph for PV Sindhu is a monumental confidence booster, signaling her return to elite form just ahead of major international assignments.
summary:
The Japan Open 2026 has concluded with **PV Sindhu** delivering a sensational performance, capturing her maiden title at this Super 750 event. Her victory, a dominant 21-17, 21-17 win over home favourite Akane Yamaguchi in the final, marks her first title since the Syed Modi India International in 2024, ending a significant drought and silencing doubters. Sindhu's path to the championship included a strong showing against World No. 4 Chen Yufei in the semi-finals and a walkover from Nozomi Okuhara, showcasing a renewed aggression and self-belief.

While Sindhu shone brightest, the rest of the Indian contingent faced early exits. Top men's doubles pair **Satwik & Chirag**, seeded third and coming in hot from their Singapore Open win, were unable to make a deep run. Similarly, **Lakshya Sen** and **HS Prannoy** in men's singles, and **Treesa & Gayatri** in women's doubles, concluded their campaigns in the initial rounds. The Japan Open served as a crucial test, and for Sindhu, it's a resounding success, signaling a powerful return to her championship-winning ways.
